texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. Kharif Season Sowing Status:
texts.text: 1. Kharif Season Sowing Status:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Kharif  sowing  data  collection  has  started from  05 th June,  2026  and  the  sowing  is  in  preliminary stage. As on 19 June 2026, the area sown under Kharif crops is 119.90 lakh ha (including 57.31 lakh ha in Sugarcane) as compared to 117.95 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text:  Kharif  sowing  data  collection  has  started from  05 th June,  2026  and  the  sowing  is  in  preliminary stage. As on 19 June 2026, the area sown under Kharif crops is 119.90 lakh ha (including 57.31 lakh ha in Sugarcane) as compared to 117.95 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Weather Update:
texts.text: 2. Weather Update:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Temperature: Maximum temperature is likely to be above normal in Madhya Maharashtra, East India, Rajasthan and many parts of Northeast India.
texts.text: Temperature: Maximum temperature is likely to be above normal in Madhya Maharashtra, East India, Rajasthan and many parts of Northeast India.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Rainfall Distribution: From June 01- 14 June 2026, the All-India cumulative rainfall is 8% lower than  its  Long  Period  Average  (LPA),  with  regional  variations:  (i)  East  &  Northeast  India: (-) 40%; (ii) Northwest India: 13%; (iii) Central India: (-) 55%; and South Peninsula: (-) 4%.
texts.text: Rainfall Distribution: From June 01- 14 June 2026, the All-India cumulative rainfall is 8% lower than  its  Long  Period  Average  (LPA),  with  regional  variations:  (i)  East  &  Northeast  India: (-) 40%; (ii) Northwest India: 13%; (iii) Central India: (-) 55%; and South Peninsula: (-) 4%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The seasonal trough at mean sea level from Punjab to Bihar across Haryana and Uttar Pradesh persists.
texts.text:  The seasonal trough at mean sea level from Punjab to Bihar across Haryana and Uttar Pradesh persists.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Very  heavy  to  extremely  Heavy  rainfall  likely  over  northeast  India  till  25th  and  over  SubHimalayan West Bengal & Sikkim till 22nd June. 2026.
texts.text:  Very  heavy  to  extremely  Heavy  rainfall  likely  over  northeast  India  till  25th  and  over  SubHimalayan West Bengal & Sikkim till 22nd June. 2026.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Moderate  to  severe  thunderstorm  activity  accompanied  by  squally  winds,  with  moderate  to severe Lightning activity is likely over East India, Chhattisgarh, and Rajasthan during the next two days.
texts.text:  Moderate  to  severe  thunderstorm  activity  accompanied  by  squally  winds,  with  moderate  to severe Lightning activity is likely over East India, Chhattisgarh, and Rajasthan during the next two days.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Duststorm activity likely over West Rajasthan on 22nd June.  Heat wave to severe Heat wave conditions likely over Vidarbha, East Madhya Pradesh, and East Uttar Pradesh for next 4-5 days and Heat Wave conditions over Chhattisgarh for next 3 days and Telangana for next 2 days.  Overall, rainfall activity is likely to remain below normal over most parts of the country except some parts of south peninsular India and north-east India where it will be near normal during the week. 3. Inputs Situation a) Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  18  June  2026,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  166  important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 50.457 billion cubic meter, which is 27.49% of total live storage capacity (183.565 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 86.62 percent of last  year's  storage  and  113.02  percent  of  the  average  storage  of  the  last  ten  years  during  the corresponding period. The overall storage position is less than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole but is better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period. ㄰⸲ㄮ㐰⸳ ㌰ⵊ啎ⴲ〲㘠㄰㨵㔠䅍
texts.text:  Duststorm activity likely over West Rajasthan on 22nd June.  Heat wave to severe Heat wave conditions likely over Vidarbha, East Madhya Pradesh, and East Uttar Pradesh for next 4-5 days and Heat Wave conditions over Chhattisgarh for next 3 days and Telangana for next 2 days.  Overall, rainfall activity is likely to remain below normal over most parts of the country except some parts of south peninsular India and north-east India where it will be near normal during the week. 3. Inputs Situation a) Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  18  June  2026,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  166  important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 50.457 billion cubic meter, which is 27.49% of total live storage capacity (183.565 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 86.62 percent of last  year's  storage  and  113.02  percent  of  the  average  storage  of  the  last  ten  years  during  the corresponding period. The overall storage position is less than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole but is better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period. ㄰⸲ㄮ㐰⸳ ㌰ⵊ啎ⴲ〲㘠㄰㨵㔠䅍

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Groundwater  Levels :  In  January,  2026,  around  75.8%  of  the  country's  monitoring  stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are observed in around 13.5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.
texts.text: Groundwater  Levels :  In  January,  2026,  around  75.8%  of  the  country's  monitoring  stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are observed in around 13.5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Key Insights
texts.text: Key Insights

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The  deeper  groundwater  level  in  the states  like  Western  Rajasthan,  Punjab,  Haryana  and Chandigarh  during  January,  2026  has  been  significantly  influenced  by  the  over  extraction  of groundwater compared to the recharge in the area, indicating negative water balance.
texts.text: The  deeper  groundwater  level  in  the states  like  Western  Rajasthan,  Punjab,  Haryana  and Chandigarh  during  January,  2026  has  been  significantly  influenced  by  the  over  extraction  of groundwater compared to the recharge in the area, indicating negative water balance.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level in January, 2026 when compared to the decadal mean water level of 2016-2025.
texts.text: About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level in January, 2026 when compared to the decadal mean water level of 2016-2025.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The  Groundwater  Drought  Indices  indicate  that  in  January,  2026,  groundwater  conditions  across India remained largely stable, with 90.30% of monitoring stations falling under normal condition in respect of Ground Water Drought. However, localized deficits were observed in several regions notably in Arunachal Pradesh, Himachal Pradesh, Meghalaya, and Nagaland.
texts.text: The  Groundwater  Drought  Indices  indicate  that  in  January,  2026,  groundwater  conditions  across India remained largely stable, with 90.30% of monitoring stations falling under normal condition in respect of Ground Water Drought. However, localized deficits were observed in several regions notably in Arunachal Pradesh, Himachal Pradesh, Meghalaya, and Nagaland.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c) Fertilizers: The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products. 4. Pests  &  Diseases :  Intensity  of  pests  and  diseases  at  some  fields  has  been  reported  to  be  above Economic  Threshold  Level  for  Maize  -vegetative  stage  (Downy  mildew (Peronosclerospora  sorghi) )  in Karnataka. The Advisory has been issued by the survey team to the farms and regular monitoring is being carried out. The overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level ㄰⸲ㄮ㐰⸳ ㌰ⵊ啎ⴲ〲㘠㄰㨵㔠䅍
texts.text: Fertilizers: The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products. 4. Pests  &  Diseases :  Intensity  of  pests  and  diseases  at  some  fields  has  been  reported  to  be  above Economic  Threshold  Level  for  Maize  -vegetative  stage  (Downy  mildew (Peronosclerospora  sorghi) )  in Karnataka. The Advisory has been issued by the survey team to the farms and regular monitoring is being carried out. The overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level ㄰⸲ㄮ㐰⸳ ㌰ⵊ啎ⴲ〲㘠㄰㨵㔠䅍

texts.orig: 5. Crops whose Mandi Wholesale prices are below MSP:
texts.text: 5. Crops whose Mandi Wholesale prices are below MSP:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: All the commodities are currently going below MSP during this week.
texts.text: All the commodities are currently going below MSP during this week.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. RAINFALL
texts.text: 1. RAINFALL

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  During the week (11 June - 17 June 2026), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 48%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st  June to 17 June 2026) is lower than LPA by 38%.
texts.text:  During the week (11 June - 17 June 2026), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 48%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st  June to 17 June 2026) is lower than LPA by 38%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Overall,  weaker  southwest  monsoon  conditions  are  likely  to  prevail  over  the  country  during  the Week.
texts.text:  Overall,  weaker  southwest  monsoon  conditions  are  likely  to  prevail  over  the  country  during  the Week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Overall, rainfall activity is likely to remain below normal over most parts of the country during the week.
texts.text:  Overall, rainfall activity is likely to remain below normal over most parts of the country during the week.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Rainfall Forecast: TEMPERATURE: MIN & MAX ㄰⸲ㄮ㐰⸳ ㌰ⵊ啎ⴲ〲㘠㄰㨵㔠䅍

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Week 1 (19 June - 25 June 2026):
texts.text: Week 1 (19 June - 25 June 2026):

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Gradual fall in maximum temperatures by about 2°C likely over Northwest India till 22nd June 2026, gradual rise by 2- 3°C for subsequent 3 days and no significant change thereafter.
texts.text:  Gradual fall in maximum temperatures by about 2°C likely over Northwest India till 22nd June 2026, gradual rise by 2- 3°C for subsequent 3 days and no significant change thereafter.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  No significant change in maximum temperatures likely over Maharashtra till 21th June and gradual fall by 2-3°C thereafter.
texts.text:  No significant change in maximum temperatures likely over Maharashtra till 21th June and gradual fall by 2-3°C thereafter.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Details of each Segment
texts.text: Details of each Segment

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Heat wave conditions very likely in isolated pockets over Chhattisgarh on 22nd & 23rd June; East Madhya  Pradesh,  Vidarbha  during  22nd-25th  June,  East  Uttar  Pradesh  during  22nd-24th  June; Telangana on 22nd June; with severe heat wave conditions in isolated pockets during 22nd-24th June.
texts.text:  Heat wave conditions very likely in isolated pockets over Chhattisgarh on 22nd & 23rd June; East Madhya  Pradesh,  Vidarbha  during  22nd-25th  June,  East  Uttar  Pradesh  during  22nd-24th  June; Telangana on 22nd June; with severe heat wave conditions in isolated pockets during 22nd-24th June.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Week 2 (26 June - 02 July 2026):
texts.text: Week 2 (26 June - 02 July 2026):

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Low  probability  of  Heatwave  conditions  likely  over  parts  of  northwest  India  (Rajasthan,  Punjab, Haryana & Uttar Pradesh), and central India (Vidarbha and adjoining regions of North Telangana) on some days of week.
texts.text:  Low  probability  of  Heatwave  conditions  likely  over  parts  of  northwest  India  (Rajasthan,  Punjab, Haryana & Uttar Pradesh), and central India (Vidarbha and adjoining regions of North Telangana) on some days of week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Above normal (+1.6 to +3.0°C) maximum temperatures are likely over many parts of the plains of northwest India, central India, north peninsular India, and East India. Near-normal weekly maximum temperatures (-1.5 to +1.5°C) are likely across the rest of the country during the week.
texts.text:  Above normal (+1.6 to +3.0°C) maximum temperatures are likely over many parts of the plains of northwest India, central India, north peninsular India, and East India. Near-normal weekly maximum temperatures (-1.5 to +1.5°C) are likely across the rest of the country during the week.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Advance of Southwest Monsoon 2026:
texts.text: Advance of Southwest Monsoon 2026:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The Northern Limit of Monsoon continues to pass through 18°N/60°E, 18°N/65°E, 18°N/70°E, Harnai, Solapur, Hyderabad, Bhadrachalam, Koraput, Phulbani, Ranchi, Jamui, Muzaffarpur and 28.3°N/83°E.
texts.text:  The Northern Limit of Monsoon continues to pass through 18°N/60°E, 18°N/65°E, 18°N/70°E, Harnai, Solapur, Hyderabad, Bhadrachalam, Koraput, Phulbani, Ranchi, Jamui, Muzaffarpur and 28.3°N/83°E.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Conditions  are  favourable  for further  advance  of  southwest  monsoon  into  some  more  parts  of Maharashtra,  Telangana,  Odisha,  Jharkhand  &  Bihar,  and  some  parts  of  Chhattisgarh  around 23rd June, 2026.
texts.text:  Conditions  are  favourable  for further  advance  of  southwest  monsoon  into  some  more  parts  of Maharashtra,  Telangana,  Odisha,  Jharkhand  &  Bihar,  and  some  parts  of  Chhattisgarh  around 23rd June, 2026.

texts.text: 2. CROPS

texts.orig: Kharif Crops - Area coverage
texts.text: Kharif Crops - Area coverage

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Sugarcane :  About 57.31 lakh ha area coverage (106% of Normal area) has been reported compared to 56.64 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text:  Sugarcane :  About 57.31 lakh ha area coverage (106% of Normal area) has been reported compared to 56.64 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Cotton :  About  17.13  lakh  ha  area  coverage  has  been  reported  compared  to  22.82  lakh  ha  during  the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text:  Cotton :  About  17.13  lakh  ha  area  coverage  has  been  reported  compared  to  22.82  lakh  ha  during  the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Jute :  About  6.22  lakh  ha  area  coverage  has  been  reported  compared  to  6.09  lakh  ha  during  the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text:  Jute :  About  6.22  lakh  ha  area  coverage  has  been  reported  compared  to  6.09  lakh  ha  during  the corresponding period of last year.

texts.orig: 3. RESERVOIR STORAGE
texts.text: 3. RESERVOIR STORAGE

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As on 18 June, 2026, the current live storage in 166 reservoirs was 50.457 BCM (27.49%) of total live capacity of 183.565 BCM.
texts.text: As on 18 June, 2026, the current live storage in 166 reservoirs was 50.457 BCM (27.49%) of total live capacity of 183.565 BCM.

texts.text:  122 reservoirs having storage more than 80% of normal storage.

texts.text:  119 reservoirs having storage 51% to 80%.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Soil Moisture Observation
texts.text: Soil Moisture Observation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Soil moisture during 13 June - 19 June 2026 is similar to the average of the past 10 years in major  parts  of  Rajasthan,  Punjab,  Uttarakhand  Uttar  Pradesh,  Haryana,  Himachal  Pradesh, Tamil Nadu, Kerala and J & K.
texts.text:  Soil moisture during 13 June - 19 June 2026 is similar to the average of the past 10 years in major  parts  of  Rajasthan,  Punjab,  Uttarakhand  Uttar  Pradesh,  Haryana,  Himachal  Pradesh, Tamil Nadu, Kerala and J & K.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Soil moisture is better than the average of the past 10 years in parts of Ladakh and pockets of eastern Rajasthan.
texts.text:  Soil moisture is better than the average of the past 10 years in parts of Ladakh and pockets of eastern Rajasthan.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  It  was  lower  than  average  of  past  10  years  in  pockets  of  Maharashtra,  Chhattisgarh,  Odisha, Northern Karnataka, Telangana, Jharkhand, Southern West Bengal, Gujarat, Eastern & southern parts of MP  and in the Northeastern parts of Country.
texts.text:  It  was  lower  than  average  of  past  10  years  in  pockets  of  Maharashtra,  Chhattisgarh,  Odisha, Northern Karnataka, Telangana, Jharkhand, Southern West Bengal, Gujarat, Eastern & southern parts of MP  and in the Northeastern parts of Country.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 5.   FERTILIZER POSITION:
texts.text: 5.   FERTILIZER POSITION:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Availability of fertilizer has been found comfortable. Details of Requirement, Receipt, Availability, Sales and Stock position of Major Fertilizers (Urea, DAP, MOP, Complex and SSP) during Kharif 2026 as on 19.06.2026 is given below:
texts.text: Availability of fertilizer has been found comfortable. Details of Requirement, Receipt, Availability, Sales and Stock position of Major Fertilizers (Urea, DAP, MOP, Complex and SSP) during Kharif 2026 as on 19.06.2026 is given below:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: (Unit : in Lakh MT)
texts.text: (Unit : in Lakh MT)

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · In case of DAP, The opening stock of Kharif 2026 as compared to the opening stock of Kharif 2025 is more by 11.78 LMT
texts.text: In case of DAP, The opening stock of Kharif 2026 as compared to the opening stock of Kharif 2025 is more by 11.78 LMT

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · As on 19 th June,  2026 the availability of DAP is 30.88 LMT against the requirement of 10.92 LMT during the ongoing Kharif 2026 season.
texts.text: As on 19 th June,  2026 the availability of DAP is 30.88 LMT against the requirement of 10.92 LMT during the ongoing Kharif 2026 season.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · As on 19 th  June 2026, adequate stock of Urea (124.43 LMT), DAP (30.88 LMT), MOP (12.45 LMT), NPK (71.98 LMT) and SSP (34.48 LMT) is available.
texts.text: As on 19 th  June 2026, adequate stock of Urea (124.43 LMT), DAP (30.88 LMT), MOP (12.45 LMT), NPK (71.98 LMT) and SSP (34.48 LMT) is available.
